question_text:
Special Binary String

Special binary strings are binary strings with the following two properties:

  • The number of 0's is equal to the number of 1's.
  • Every prefix of the binary string has at least as many 1's as 0's.

You are given a special binary string s. A move consists of choosing two consecutive, non-empty, special substrings of s, and swapping them. Return the lexicographically largest resulting string after any number of moves.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= s.length <= 50
  • s[i] is either '0' or '1'.
  • s is a special binary string.
output_format: The lexicographically largest string achievable by swapping consecutive special substrings.
constraints: s is a valid special binary string
explanation:
Recursive Decomposition

A special binary string starts with 1 and ends with 0. Decompose s into maximal special substrings S1, S2, ..., Sk. Recursively maximize each inner part (strip the outer 1 and 0). Sort the results in descending order (greedy). Concatenate their maximized forms.

Time: O(N² log N). Space: O(N).
